description_long: This awning features a large, square, pitched roof made of what appears to be thatched or synthetic straw material, providing ample shade and weather protection. The canopy is supported by four slender vertical posts at each corner, with additional horizontal slats forming partial walls on two sides, enhancing structural stability and offering some privacy or wind shielding. The roof has a subtle central peak, and the overhanging eaves extend well beyond the support posts, maximizing the shaded area beneath. The structure is open on two sides, allowing easy access and visibility, making it suitable for use in parks, outdoor cafes, or public gathering spaces. The materials and construction evoke a tropical or rustic aesthetic, blending natural tones with functional design. No branding, signage, or decorative trim is visible, emphasizing a simple, utilitarian style.
description: A large, square, thatched-roof awning with open sides and partial slatted walls, supported by four posts. The design is rustic and functional, ideal for outdoor urban spaces.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the awning, with the slatted wall on the left and open access beneath the canopy.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the structure, the slatted wall on the near side, and the overhanging roof.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the opposite slatted wall and the open area beneath the canopy.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, showing the slatted wall on the right and the open structure beneath the roof.
attribute_mounting_location: freestanding on flat ground or pavement
attribute_edge_trim_style: straight edge with no decorative trim
attribute_support_structure_type: fixed frame with four vertical posts and horizontal slatted walls
